Title: Two reductases complete steroidal glycoalkaloids biosynthesis in potato

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: solanidane steroidal glycoalkaloid terminal conversion branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH-dependent reduction in the terminal conversion of zwittersolanine/zwitterchaconine, primarily converting zwittersolanine to 16-iminiumsolanine and zwitterchaconine to 16-iminiumchaconine; also reduces 22-iminiumsolanine/22-iminiumchaconine to α-solanine/α-chaconine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate intermediates and can directly form the target product from one iminium intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal-pathway enzyme completing the final two-step reductive conversion to major potato SGAs; not established as sole rate-limiting enzyme. The RPG1-first route appears predominant due to higher reaction efficiency than RPG2-first route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with RPG2 for α-solanine and α-chaconine accumulation; promotes conversion of zwitter intermediates toward toxic terminal SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Toxic defense metabolites; associated with resistance to diseases and pests, but undesirable for food safety due to toxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: potato hairy roots; potato tuber sprouts/tubers discussed in study context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sassy (for cDNA cloning); transgenic potato hairy roots cultivar not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified by co-expression with known SGA biosynthetic genes across potato tissues and treatments; cDNA amplified from tuber sprouts.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NmrA-like protein; contains nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (phosphate)-binding motif; structurally related to short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-like proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout in potato hairy roots; recombinant protein enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling; reverse reaction assays; NMR structural elucidation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPG1 was identified as a co-expressed candidate within the potato SGA biosynthetic module. 2. RPG1/RPG2 double knockout abolished α-solanine and α-chaconine accumulation and caused zwittersolanine/zwitterchaconine accumulation in potato hairy roots. 3. Recombinant RPG1 converted zwittersolanine to 16-iminiumsolanine and zwitterchaconine to 16-iminiumchaconine. 4. RPG1 also converted 22-iminiumsolanine/22-iminiumchaconine to α-solanine/α-chaconine. 5. Reaction time-course indicated the RPG1-first route is faster and likely predominant in potato.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for reducing potato SGA toxicity by knockout/editing together with RPG2; can be leveraged to tailor SGA structures for safer crops or enhanced pest/disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing RPG1 function is expected to lower toxic SGAs but may compromise natural defense and alter SGA profile toward zwitter intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RPG1 shares 87.6% amino acid identity with RPG2. Because of high sequence similarity, individual knockout lines were not obtained; in planta evidence is from double knockout only. Likely acts in the major route via 16-iminium intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: solanidane steroidal glycoalkaloid terminal conversion branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH-dependent reduction in the terminal conversion of zwittersolanine/zwitterchaconine, converting zwittersolanine to 22-iminiumsolanine and zwitterchaconine to 22-iminiumchaconine; also reduces 16-iminiumsolanine/16-iminiumchaconine to α-solanine/α-chaconine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate intermediates and can directly form the target product from one iminium intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal-pathway enzyme completing the final two-step reductive conversion to major potato SGAs; the RPG2-first route is minor due to lower reaction efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with RPG1 for α-solanine and α-chaconine accumulation; promotes conversion of zwitter intermediates toward toxic terminal SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Toxic defense metabolites; associated with resistance to diseases and pests, but undesirable for food safety due to toxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: potato hairy roots; potato tuber/tuber sprout context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sassy (for cDNA cloning); transgenic potato hairy roots cultivar not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified by co-expression with known SGA biosynthetic genes across potato tissues and treatments; cDNA amplified from tuber sprouts.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NmrA-like protein; contains nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (phosphate)-binding motif; structurally related to short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-like proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout in potato hairy roots; recombinant protein enzyme assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling; reverse reaction assays; NMR structural elucidation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPG2 was identified as a co-expressed candidate within the potato SGA biosynthetic module. 2. RPG1/RPG2 double knockout abolished α-solanine and α-chaconine accumulation and caused zwittersolanine/zwitterchaconine accumulation in potato hairy roots. 3. Recombinant RPG2 converted zwittersolanine to 22-iminiumsolanine and zwitterchaconine to 22-iminiumchaconine. 4. RPG2 also converted 16-iminiumsolanine/16-iminiumchaconine to α-solanine/α-chaconine. 5. Time-course assays showed RPG2 acts more slowly than RPG1 in the initial conversion from zwittersolanine, indicating a minor alternative route.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for editing together with RPG1 to lower toxic SGAs or engineer alternative SGA profiles for defense-related breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing RPG2 function is expected to lower toxic SGAs but may weaken defense and alter flux toward accumulated zwitter intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RPG2 shares 87.6% amino acid identity with RPG1. High sequence similarity prevented successful generation of individual knockout lines; in planta evidence is from double knockout. Functions in a minor alternative route when acting first from zwitter intermediates.
